flow_overview: An online bookstore flow that supports homepage browsing and advanced discovery of books through metadata-based search filters.
capability_summary: Search submission from an advanced search form
Persistent storefront navigation and shopping context

requirements.text: The system shall support discovery of books through homepage browsing sections and category navigation.
requirements.rationale: The homepage shows category navigation, bestseller areas, language-specific sections, and multiple book listings, which directly ground a browsing capability separate from targeted search.

requirements.text: The system shall provide a dedicated advanced search workflow for finding books using structured search criteria.
requirements.rationale: The homepage includes an Advanced Search entry point, and the next screen is a dedicated Advanced Search page, clearly showing a separate workflow for structured discovery.

requirements.text: The system shall allow users to search for books using multiple bibliographic fields, including keyword, title, author, publisher, and ISBN.
requirements.rationale: The advanced search form visibly exposes these fields together, indicating that the search feature supports structured book metadata rather than only a single free-text query.

requirements.text: The system shall allow users to search for books by author name within the advanced search workflow.
requirements.rationale: The form contains an Author field, and later steps show that field populated with an author value, directly grounding author-based search support.

requirements.text: The system shall allow users to constrain advanced search by language.
requirements.rationale: The advanced search page includes a Language selector, and the later step shows a specific language choice applied, which directly supports language filtering.

requirements.text: The system shall allow users to combine multiple advanced search criteria in a single search request, including author and language together.
requirements.rationale: The ordered steps show the author criterion already entered and then the language criterion being set as well, indicating a combined filter configuration rather than isolated single-field searching.

requirements.text: The system shall provide an explicit action to submit configured advanced search criteria.
requirements.rationale: A dedicated Search button is visible on the advanced search page throughout the configuration steps, grounding a clear submission action for the form.

requirements.text: The system shall keep configured advanced search criteria visibly reflected in the search form before submission.
requirements.rationale: The entered author value and selected language remain visible in the form state, supporting user awareness of the current query configuration before executing the search.

requirements.text: The system shall display books matching the submitted advanced search filters so the user can browse filtered results.
requirements.rationale: The visible flow shows setup of a structured query and a search submission control, and the task is explicitly about browsing books by a given author in a given language, so a filtered results view is strongly implied even though it is not shown.

requirements.text: The system shall preserve global storefront context such as account access, order status, wishlist, cart, and locale controls while the user performs search tasks.
requirements.rationale: The same top-level storefront controls remain visible across the homepage and advanced search screens, indicating continuity of broader shopping context during discovery tasks.

requirements.text: The system shall support user-selectable locale settings relevant to shopping, including interface language and currency, alongside product discovery features.
requirements.rationale: Language and currency controls are visible in the shared header across the flow, which grounds a storefront capability for locale-related preferences while searching or browsing.

requirements.text: The system shall carry active advanced search filters forward into subsequent browsing views so users can understand the basis of the shown results.
requirements.rationale: The flow emphasizes explicit configuration of author and language before search, making it a disciplined and nearby inference that those active filters should remain understandable when results are shown, even though the results page is not visible here.

requirements.text: The system shall retain previously entered advanced search criteria when the user returns from a filtered browsing view to the advanced search workflow during the same session.
requirements.rationale:
Contrastive import from staged bundle
Intended label: partially_fulfilled
Mutation family: persistence_extension
Source gold requirement: REQ-08
Source gold text: The system shall keep configured advanced search criteria visibly reflected in the search form before submission.
Generation rationale: This is a realistic workflow-consistency extension of visible criteria reflection. The flow already shows criteria being carried within the search form, so extending that behavior to returning from results stays close to the same feature space while adding a later-screen persistence expectation that may only be partially evidenced.
requirements.parent_harvest_text: The system shall keep configured advanced search criteria visibly reflected in the search form before submission.

requirements.text: The system shall remember a signed-in user's preferred storefront locale and language-related search context for later visits to the search workflow.
requirements.rationale:
Contrastive import from staged bundle
Intended label: partially_fulfilled
Mutation family: policy_or_role_extension
Source gold requirement: REQ-11
Source gold text: The system shall support user-selectable locale settings relevant to shopping, including interface language and currency, alongside product discovery features.
Generation rationale: Locale controls and account-related storefront context are already visible, making remembered preferences a plausible account-bound extension. It stays near the accepted shopping and search context requirements, but persistence across visits depends on hidden state and would likely only be partially settled from the flow.
requirements.parent_harvest_text: The system shall support user-selectable locale settings relevant to shopping, including interface language and currency, alongside product discovery features.

requirements.text: The system shall ensure that books shown after advanced search satisfy all submitted visible constraints, including the selected author and language.
requirements.rationale:
Contrastive import from staged bundle
Intended label: abstain
Mutation family: hidden_state_extension
Source gold requirement: REQ-09
Source gold text: The system shall display books matching the submitted advanced search filters so the user can browse filtered results.
Generation rationale: This keeps the same search-to-results capability but strengthens it from visible filtering support to actual correctness of returned items. That is highly realistic for the feature, yet later verification often cannot fully prove that all shown results truly satisfy the submitted constraints from screenshots alone.
requirements.parent_harvest_text: The system shall display books matching the submitted advanced search filters so the user can browse filtered results.

requirements.text: The system shall include all currently available catalog titles that match the configured author and German-language criteria in the resulting browsing view.
requirements.rationale:
Contrastive import from staged bundle
Intended label: abstain
Mutation family: completeness_or_universal_quantifier
Source gold requirement: REQ-05
Source gold text: The system shall allow users to constrain advanced search by language.
Generation rationale: This is a disciplined completeness strengthening of the visible language-filtering capability in the exact same search space. It remains plausible for a bookstore search feature, but universal coverage of all matching titles is usually not fully decidable from the visible flow and is therefore useful for abstention pressure.
requirements.parent_harvest_text: The system shall allow users to constrain advanced search by language.

requirements.text: The system shall provide a dedicated review state before executing advanced search in which the configured bibliographic and language criteria can be checked, edited, or cleared.
requirements.rationale:
Contrastive import from staged bundle
Intended label: not_fulfilled
Mutation family: missing_visible_step
Source gold requirement: REQ-07
Source gold text: The system shall provide an explicit action to submit configured advanced search criteria.
Generation rationale: This stays tightly anchored to the existing advanced-search submission workflow but adds a plausible intermediate review phase that many systems could support. It is useful contrast because the accepted gold set shows direct configuration and submission, not an explicit separate review step.
requirements.parent_harvest_text: The system shall provide an explicit action to submit configured advanced search criteria.

requirements.text: The system shall expose active author and language filters in the results view as inline, directly editable controls so users can refine the search without reopening the full advanced search form.
requirements.rationale:
Contrastive import from staged bundle
Intended label: not_fulfilled
Mutation family: stronger_visible_constraint
Source gold requirement: REQ-12
Source gold text: The system shall carry active advanced search filters forward into subsequent browsing views so users can understand the basis of the shown results.
Generation rationale: This is a stronger, still realistic extension of carried-forward filters: not just visible persistence, but directly editable result-level refinement. It remains in the same discovery and search workflow while pushing toward a richer visible outcome that is likely absent from a minimal advanced-search flow.
requirements.parent_harvest_text: The system shall carry active advanced search filters forward into subsequent browsing views so users can understand the basis of the shown results.
